commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(routing): prevent Qwen hallucination loops

Three fixes:
1. Add stop=["<|im_start|>"] to routing call — prevents model from
   generating past JSON into fake conversation continuations
2. Reduce router memory from 10+5+3 to 5+2+1 messages — smaller
   context = less confusion for 3B model
3. Reduce routing max_tokens from 500 to 200 — routing JSON is ~80
   tokens, 500 allowed extensive hallucination loops

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(wasm): fire-and-forget JS tool registration (#120)

* feat(wasm): add fire-and-forget JS tool registration

Add `register_js_tool` WASM export for tools that return "acknowledged"
immediately without requiring a JS callback. The agent loop continues
without blocking, and the host watches `ToolCallRequested` events in the
event stream to capture args and act asynchronously. Human responses
come back via `mob_send_message`.

Existing `register_tool_callback` (callback-based) is unchanged.

Rust: JsToolMode enum (Callback | FireAndForget), register_js_tool export
TypeScript: WasmModule.register_js_tool, MeerkatRuntime.registerFireAndForgetTool

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(wasm): enforce latest-wins for duplicate JS tool names

Both register_tool_callback and register_js_tool now remove any existing
entry with the same name before inserting. This makes mode resolution
deterministic (latest registration wins) instead of order-dependent
first-match behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: cross-agent diff review before auto-merge (#37)

* feat: cross-agent diff review before auto-merge

When a coding agent completes a task successfully, spawn a different
agent to review the git diff before merging to main. Claude codes →
Codex reviews, Codex codes → Claude reviews.

- Add reviewDiffWithAgent() to AgentRunner with structured JSON prompt
- Add pickReviewAgent() for cross-agent selection logic
- Add parseReviewResponse() with non-greedy regex for robust JSON extraction
- Insert review gate in scheduler's executeAndRelease() between task
  success and pool.release(merge)
- Review runs in /tmp to prevent reviewer from modifying the worktree
- Falls back to heuristic reviewDiff() if agent fails or times out
- Add ReviewResult.approve field to gate merge decisions
- Add task.review field to persist review results
- 14 new tests (299 total, 0 failures)

Closes #37

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(review): address round-2 review issues

- Remove unused `cwd` param from reviewDiffWithAgent()
- Fix JSON extraction: use indexOf/lastIndexOf instead of regex
  (handles braces in strings correctly)
- Add task.error message when review rejects merge
- Add review_started event assertions to scheduler tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(review): extract normalizeReviewObj, add reviewAgent to result

- Extract duplicated JSON normalisation into normalizeReviewObj()
- Add reviewAgent field to ReviewResult so consumers know which agent reviewed
- Update Task.review type to include reviewAgent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(review): move ReviewResult to types.ts, add missing tests

- Move ReviewResult interface to types.ts (single source of truth)
- Task.review now references ReviewResult instead of inline duplicate
- Re-export ReviewResult from agent-runner.ts for backward compat
- Add test: normalizeReviewObj returns null for missing/wrong-type fields
- Add test: scheduler catch block when reviewDiffWithAgent throws

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ClassEval: class-level code generation with method dependencies (100 classes,
410 methods). Tests incremental N-step pipelines where later methods depend on
earlier ones — text context grows linearly, KV-cache stays fixed.

DebugBench: 2-agent Detector→Fixer bug fixing (1,414 Python instances, 18 bug
types). Tests whether latent transfer preserves bug analysis better than text
summaries.

Both benchmarks support direct/text/latent modes and follow the standard
benchmark pattern for cloud runner compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(warehouse): support partial quantity reservation with row splitting

Previously, batchReserve marked the entire warehouse_items row as reserved
regardless of how many pieces were needed. If a row had qty=6 and only 1
piece was requested, all 6 got reserved.

Now batchReserve accepts items with quantities: {itemId, quantity}[].
When requested qty < row qty, the row is split:
- Original row: quantity reduced by requested amount (stays available)
- New row: created with requested quantity (marked as reserved)

When requested qty >= row qty, the whole row is reserved (unchanged behavior).

Updated all call-sites to pass quantity from warehouseSources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: always compress traces and delete raw .jsonl on close (#7)

* fix: always compress traces and delete raw .jsonl on close

The raw .jsonl file is a temporary buffer during execution. After the
tracer closes, split_and_compress produces .jsonl.gz + optional
.content.jsonl.zst which are strictly better (same data, ~40x smaller).
Previously the raw file was only cleaned up for auto-path tracers with
backends, causing massive file bloat for explicit-path tracers.

Changes:
- tracer.py: delete raw .jsonl after successful compression in
  _prepare_for_upload, regardless of auto_path or backend config
- compress.py: clean up partial .jsonl.gz and .content.jsonl.zst on
  failure so the raw file remains the sole source of truth (previously
  partial compressed files were left on disk and the UI would prefer
  them over the complete raw file)
- tests: update assertions from .jsonl to .jsonl.gz, update read_events
  helper to transparently handle both formats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update references to reflect compressed-only output

Raw .jsonl is now a temporary buffer deleted after compression.
Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, SKILL.md, and docstrings in backend.py,
tracer.py, and compress.py to reflect that .jsonl.gz is the sole
persistent output format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clean up dead code in _schedule_cleanup and fix file handle leak

- _schedule_cleanup: remove redundant raw_path deletion (raw .jsonl is
  already deleted in _prepare_for_upload, only compressed files need
  cleanup after backend upload)
- compress.py: keep a reference to the underlying file object and pass
  closefd=True to stream_writer so it closes the file descriptor when
  the zstd writer is closed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: don't catch unlink errors in compression fallback path

Move the raw file deletion and path building outside the try/except so
the except only catches actual compression failures. Previously, if
compression succeeded but unlink raised, the fallback would return the
raw path even though compressed files already existed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
B-58: Force Gemini JSON output via responseMimeType

Gemini returned plain text (zero JSON braces) ~27% of the time,
causing enrichment failures despite B-30 fence-stripping fix.
Root cause: geminiComplete() never requested JSON mode.

Fix: Added responseMimeType: "application/json" to Gemini
generationConfig in AIProviderAdapter.js. Added debug logging
of raw response on parse failure. Made fence regex case-insensitive.

commit.message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stampede guard for concurrent same-ticker analysis runs

Two simultaneous analyses of the same ticker both miss cache before
either can write, causing independent yfinance fetches that return
slightly different forward P/E / analyst estimates → divergent EV
and valuation gap outputs.

Fix: double-check cache after fetching (financial_statements +
price_snapshot). If a concurrent request already wrote while we were
fetching, adopt its data — "first writer wins". Both runs then use
identical inputs → identical computed metrics.

Dynamic OpenAPI-driven client, bump version to 1.0.0

Replace 724 lines of hardcoded methods/dataclasses with a dynamic client
that builds itself from the OpenAPI spec at runtime. Methods are auto-generated,
responses are returned as dicts (with as_df=True for DataFrames), and the spec
is cached locally with 1hr TTL. Breaking change: dataclasses removed, return
types changed from typed objects to dicts.
